title: पृथ्वी सूक्तम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री पृथ्वी सूक्तम् ॥

ॐ भूमिर्भूम्ना द्यौर्वरिणान्तरिक्षं महित्वा।
उपस्थे ते देव्यदितेऽग्निमन्नादमन्नाद्यायादधे॥ १॥

सत्यं बृहदृतमुग्रं दीक्षा तपो ब्रह्म यज्ञः पृथिवीं धारयन्ति।
सा नो भूतस्य भव्यस्य पत्न्युरुं लोकं पृथिवी नः कृणोतु॥ २॥

असंबाधं मध्यतो मानवानां यस्या उद्वतः प्रवतः समं बहु।
नानावीर्या ओषधीर्या बिभर्ति पृथिवी नः प्रथतां राध्यतां नः॥ ३॥

यस्यां समुद्र उत सिन्धुरापो यस्यामन्नं कृष्टयः सम्बभूवुः।
यस्यामिदं जिन्वति प्राणदेजत् सा नो भूमिः पूर्वपेये दधातु॥ ४॥

यस्याश्चतस्रः प्रदिशः पृथिव्या यस्यामन्नं कृष्टयः सम्बभूवुः।
या बिभर्ति बहुधा प्राणतो जनान् सा नो भूमिर्गोष्वप्यन्ने दधातु॥ ५॥

यस्यां पूर्वे पूर्वजना विचक्रिरे यस्यां देवा असुरानभ्यवर्तयन्।
गवामश्वानां वयसश्च विष्ठा भगं वर्चः पृथिवी नो दधातु॥ ६॥

विश्वम्भरा वसुधानी प्रतिष्ठा हिरण्यवक्षा जगतो निवेशनी।
वैश्वानरं बिभ्रती भूमिरग्निमिन्द्रऋषभा द्रविणे नो दधातु॥ ७॥

यां रक्षन्त्यस्वप्ना विश्वदानीं देवा भूमिं पृथिवीमप्रमादम्।
सा नो मधु प्रियं दुहामथो उक्षतु वर्चसा॥ ८॥

यत्ते भूमे विखनामि क्षिप्रं तदपि रोहतु।
मा ते मर्म विमृग्वरि मा ते हृदयमर्पिपम्॥ ९॥

ग्रीष्मस्ते भूमे वर्षाणि शरद्धेमन्तः शिशिरो वसन्तः।
ऋतवस्ते विहिताः संवत्सरः कालो रात्र्याह्ना च विश्वं भूमे पत्यतां नः॥ १०॥

माता भूमिः पुत्रोऽहं पृथिव्याः पर्जन्यः पिता स उ नः पिपर्तु।
ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥ ११॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Prithvi Suktam ||

Om Bhumir-bhumna dyaur-varinantar-iksham mahitva |
Upasthe te devy-Adite Agnim-annadam-annadyaya-dadhe || 1 ||

Satyam brihad-ritam-ugram diksha tapo Brahma yajnah prithivim dharayanti |
Sa no bhutasya bhavyasya patny-urum lokam prithivi nah krinotu || 2 ||

Asambadham madhyato manavanam yasya udvatah pravatah samam bahu |
Nana-virya oshadhir-ya bibharti prithivi nah prathatam radhyatam nah || 3 ||

Yasyam samudra uta sindhur-apo yasyam-annam krishtayah sambabhuvuh |
Yasyam-idam jinvati pranad-ejat sa no bhumih purva-peye dadhatu || 4 ||

Yasyash-chatasrah pradishash prithivya yasyam-annam krishtayah sambabhuvuh |
Ya bibharti bahudha pranato janan sa no bhumir-goshv-apy-anne dadhatu || 5 ||

Yasyam purve purvajana vichakrire yasyam deva asuran-abhy-avartayan |
Gavam-ashvanam vayasash-cha vishtha bhagam varchah prithivi no dadhatu || 6 ||

Vishvambhara vasudhani pratishtha hiranyavaksha jagato niveshani |
Vaishvanaram bibhrati bhumir-Agnim-Indra-rishabha draviṇe no dadhatu || 7 ||

Yam rakshantay-asvapna vishvadanim deva bhumim prithivim-apramadam |
Sa no madhu priyam duham-atho ukshatu varchasaa || 8 ||

Yat-te bhume vikhanami kshipram tad-api rohatu |
Ma te marma vimrigvari ma te hridayam-arpipam || 9 ||

Grishmaste bhume varshani sharad-dhemantah shishiro vasantah |
Ritavaste vihitah samvatsarah kalo ratry-ahna cha vishvam bhume patyatam nah || 10 ||

Mata bhumih putro-aham prithivyah Parjanyah pita sa u nah pipartu |
Om Shantih Shantih Shantih || 11 ||
hindiMeaning:
पृथ्वी अपने विस्तार से, द्यौ अपने आवरण से, अन्तरिक्ष अपनी महत्ता से। हे देवी अदिति! आपकी गोद में अन्नाद अग्नि को अन्नाद्य के लिए स्थापित करता हूँ॥ १॥

सत्य, बृहत, ऋत, उग्र, दीक्षा, तप, ब्रह्म और यज्ञ — ये पृथ्वी को धारण करते हैं। वह भूत और भविष्य की स्वामिनी, हमें विशाल लोक प्रदान करे॥ २॥

मनुष्यों के मध्य में जो अबाधित है, जिसके ऊँचे-नीचे और समतल अनेक भाग हैं, जो नाना प्रकार की वीर्यवान औषधियाँ धारण करती है — पृथ्वी हमें विस्तार और सम्पन्नता दे॥ ३॥

जिसमें समुद्र, सिन्धु, जल हैं, जिसमें अन्न और कृषि उत्पन्न हुई, जिसमें यह सब प्राणवान चेतन जीवित है — वह भूमि हमें प्रथम पान (पोषण) दे॥ ४॥

जिसमें पूर्वज और प्राचीन लोगों ने कर्म किया, जिसमें देवों ने असुरों को पराजित किया — गौ, अश्व, पक्षियों का भाग्य और तेज पृथ्वी हमें प्रदान करे॥ ६॥

विश्वम्भरा, वसुधारिणी, प्रतिष्ठा, हिरण्यवक्षा, जगत की निवासदात्री — वैश्वानर अग्नि धारण करने वाली भूमि, इन्द्र-श्रेष्ठा, हमें धन दे॥ ७॥

हे भूमि! मैं जो तुम्हें खोदता हूँ, वह शीघ्र फिर उग आए। तुम्हारे मर्मस्थल को मैं न बेधूँ, तुम्हारे हृदय को पीड़ित न करूँ॥ ९॥

माता भूमि है, मैं पृथ्वी का पुत्र हूँ। पर्जन्य (वर्षा) पिता है — वे हमारा पालन करें॥ ११॥
benefits: Sacred hymn to Mother Earth — one of the greatest ecological prayers in any scripture
Invokes Earth as the nurturing, all-bearing mother of humanity
Invokes agricultural prosperity and abundance of food grains
Teaches reverence for the environment and natural resources
Powerful for Bhoomi Puja, land worship, and Vastu ceremonies
Promotes ecological balance and environmental consciousness
Traditionally associated with stability, grounding, and material prosperity
description: The Prithvi Suktam, known in Sanskrit as पृथ्वी सूक्तम्, is a Vedic hymn in praise of Lord Vishnu. Drawn from Atharva Veda (Kanda 12, Sukta 1), this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to teach reverence for the environment and natural resources and invoke Earth as the nurturing, all-bearing mother of humanity. It is also said to bring stability, grounding, and material prosperity. It is especially recommended on Thursday (Guruvar) for maximum spiritual benefit. At 11 verses long, the recitation takes roughly 8 minutes, making it accessible for regular devotional use. On this page, you can read the complete Prithvi Suktam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Prithvi Suktam and where does it appear in the Vedas?
seoFaqs.answer: The Prithvi Suktam (पृथ्वी सूक्तम् — prithvi = earth) is the great Vedic Hymn to the Earth, preserved in the Atharva Veda — Kanda 12, Sukta 1 — comprising 63 verses, making it one of the longest single hymns in the entire Atharva Veda. It is the most extensive ancient meditation on the Earth as a living, divine, conscious mother, and is regarded today as humanity's earliest environmental and ecological text. The hymn is also known as the Bhumi Sukta and is recited daily by traditionalists, environmentalists and Vastu practitioners as a prayer for forgiveness from the Earth, for harmonious dwelling upon her, and for the welfare of all that grows from her. (Sources: Atharva Veda Kanda 12 Sukta 1; Hindupedia Bhumi Sukta; Vedic environmental tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the meaning of Mata Bhumih Putro Aham Prithivyah?
seoFaqs.answer: This celebrated verse from the Prithvi Suktam (Atharva Veda 12.1.12) declares: Mata Bhumih putro aham prithivyah — The Earth is my mother, and I am her son. This single line is among the most quoted verses from the entire Atharva Veda and forms the philosophical foundation of Hindu environmental ethics and Vasundhara Vandana (Earth-veneration). The verse establishes a familial relationship between human beings and the Earth — not one of ownership, exploitation or use, but of filial reverence. The Earth is not a resource but a mother; humans are not masters but children. This perspective predates by three millennia the modern Gaia hypothesis and indigenous earth-mother spiritualities elsewhere. (Sources: Atharva Veda 12.1.12; classical Vedic exegesis; Hindupedia Bhumi.)

seoFaqs.question: Why is the Prithvi Suktam considered the world's earliest environmental hymn?
seoFaqs.answer: Composed in approximately the late second millennium BCE, the Prithvi Suktam predates every other surviving environmental or ecological text in human literature. Its 63 verses cover a remarkable range of environmental themes: the Earth as a living conscious being with feelings, the diversity of her landscapes (mountains, plains, snow, deserts, forests, rivers), her sustaining role for all life-forms (plants, animals, humans, devas), the moral duty of humans not to harm her, prayers for forgiveness for the inevitable injuries we cause by walking, ploughing and dwelling, the harmony of seasons, the support of biodiversity, prayers against pollution of waters and soil, and explicit invocations for ecological balance. UN environmental conferences and modern eco-spirituality movements regularly cite the Prithvi Sukta as the foundational Hindu environmental scripture. (Sources: Atharva Veda 12.1; Hindupedia Vedic ecology; UN environmental literature.)

seoFaqs.question: When and how is the Prithvi Suktam traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Prithvi Suktam is most powerfully recited on every Amavasya (new moon, sacred to Bhumi Devi and ancestors), every Bhumi Pujan ceremony (the Vedic ritual before commencing any construction, agricultural or earth-disturbing activity), Vasundhara Vrata, Bhumi Vrata, on Earth Day (April 22 — adopted by modern Hindu environmental movements), at the start of any ploughing season, before Griha-Pravesha (housewarming), during Vastu Shanti, before any construction or excavation, daily by traditional farmers before entering the field, and during Pancha Suktam parayanam (where Bhu Sukta is the fourth of the five suktas). It is also recited as a prayer for forgiveness whenever excavation, deforestation or earth-injury cannot be avoided. (Sources: Vastu Shastra paddhati; Bhumi Pujan manuals; Drikpanchang.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Prithvi Suktam and Bhu Suktam?
seoFaqs.answer: Both invoke the Earth Goddess but they are distinct hymns from different Vedic sources. The Prithvi Suktam (पृथ्वी सूक्तम्) is the longer 63-verse hymn from the Atharva Veda Kanda 12 Sukta 1, with extensive cosmological, ecological and philosophical content. The Bhu Suktam (भू सूक्तम्) is a shorter Krishna Yajurveda hymn (from the Taittiriya Samhita and Aranyaka tradition) used as the fourth of the Pancha Suktam in Vaishnava Vishnu abhisheka, focused specifically on Bhumi Devi as the consort of Vishnu. The Prithvi Sukta is more cosmic, environmental and Atharvan; the Bhu Sukta is more devotional, Vaishnava and abhisheka-focused. Both are recited at major Bhumi Puja ceremonies. (Sources: Atharva Veda 12.1; Taittiriya Samhita; Vaishnava paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits and applications are attributed to Prithvi Suktam recitation?
seoFaqs.answer: Tradition attributes the following benefits to regular Prithvi Suktam recitation: harmony with the land on which one dwells (Vastu shanti), removal of bhumi-dosha (negative earth energies), success in agriculture and yields, protection from earthquakes and earth-related disasters, stability of property and real estate, reverence for environment leading to spiritual merit (eco-punya), forgiveness for the inevitable injuries caused to soil and plants by daily living, harmonious griha-pravesha and habitation, fertility of land for farmers, success in mining and excavation when accompanied by reparative offerings, blessings of Bhumi Devi for childbirth (since the Earth is the cosmic mother of all living beings), and the supreme phala — alignment with the rita (cosmic order) of the Earth and its seasons. (Sources: Atharva Veda 12.1 phalashruti; Bhumi Pujan paddhati; Vastu tradition.)
